referenceState: Reference state: uf = 0 kJ/kg and sf = 0 kJ/(kg·K) for saturated liquid at the triple point (T = 0.01°C, P = 0.0006117 MPa) — confirmed directly from the first row of the extracted saturated-temperature data, not assumed by analogy to other fluids. Note hf at this state is 0.001 kJ/kg, not exactly 0, because h = u + Pv and the Pv term is nonzero (though tiny) at the triple point.
sourceNote: Extracted from standard thermodynamics textbook appendix tables (Saturated water — Temperature and Pressure tables); cross-verified against published IAPWS-IF97-traceable steam table references (8 points spanning low/mid/high/critical range; largest deviation ~0.03%). Not verified against NIST WebBook's interactive fluid property calculator — that tool requires form/session state and could not be reached via a plain fetch, so it was not used as a source.
